source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Two squares $9 \mathrm{~cm} \times 9 \mathrm{~cm}$ overlap so as to form a rectangle $9 \mathrm{~cm} \times 13 \mathrm{~cm}$ as shown. Find the area of the region in which the two squares overlap.
<image1>
extraction_notes: answer field is option letter; mapped to option text using options array

image_dependency: question_text includes 'as shown', so the exact geometric configuration is specified by the diagram
The task depends on spatial overlap and shape arrangement, which are visual/geometric properties
multi_step: A valid solution requires combining the areas of two 9x9 squares with the area of the resulting 9x13 rectangle
This is at least a two-step reasoning process: compute known areas, then use subtraction/inclusion-exclusion to infer the overlap
The problem is not a direct single-formula lookup because the overlap must be derived from the composite figure
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the target is a single numeric area value
Multiple-choice options are provided, which makes final-answer checking straightforward
Given the stated dimensions and fixed diagram configuration, the overlap area can be independently recomputed

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question text, answer, and required diagram are all present.
Registry passed with no recorded issues, so there is no obvious asset integrity risk.
The sample has high image dependency, but the necessary image exists, making the diagram-dependent semantics recoverable.
High verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value make it a strong candidate for the pool.
